secrets.title: List organization secrets

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Lists all secrets available in an organization without revealing their encrypted values.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

items.title: Actions Secret for an Organization
items.description: Secrets for GitHub Actions for an organization.

secrets.title: Get an organization public key

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Gets your public key, which you need to encrypt secrets. You need to encrypt a secret before you can create or update secrets.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

schema.title: ActionsPublicKey
schema.description: The public key used for setting Actions Secrets.

secrets.title: Get an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Gets a single organization secret without revealing its encrypted value.

The authenticated user must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

schema.title: Actions Secret for an Organization
schema.description: Secrets for GitHub Actions for an organization.

secrets.title: Create or update an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

bodyParameters.name: encrypted_value
bodyParameters.description: Value for your secret, encrypted with LibSodium using the public key retrieved from the Get an organization public key endpoint.

bodyParameters.name: key_id
bodyParameters.description: ID of the key you used to encrypt the secret.

bodyParameters.name: visibility
bodyParameters.description: Which type of organization repositories have access to the organization secret. selected means only the repositories specified by selected_repository_ids can access the secret.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository ids that can access the organization secret. You can only provide a list of repository ids when the visibility is set to selected. You can manage the list of selected repositories using the List selected repositories for an organization secret, Set selected repositories for an organization secret, and Remove selected repository from an organization secret endpoints.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Creates or updates an organization secret with an encrypted value. Encrypt your secret using LibSodium. For more information, see "Encrypting secrets for the REST API."

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

secrets.title: Delete an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Deletes a secret in an organization using the secret name.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

secrets.title: List selected repositories for an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Lists all repositories that have been selected when the visibility for repository access to a secret is set to selected.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

advanced_security.description:
Enable or disable GitHub Advanced Security for the repository.

For standalone Code Scanning or Secret Protection products, this parameter cannot be used.

dependabot_security_updates.description: Enable or disable Dependabot security updates for the repository.

status.description: The enablement status of Dependabot security updates for the repository.

reviewers.description: The bypass reviewers for secret scanning delegated bypass

reviewer_id.description: The ID of the team or role selected as a bypass reviewer

reviewer_type.description: The type of the bypass reviewer

mode.description: The bypass mode for the reviewer

custom_properties.description: The custom properties that were defined for the repository. The keys are the custom property names, and the values are the corresponding custom property values.

secrets.title: Set selected repositories for an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository ids that can access the organization secret. You can only provide a list of repository ids when the visibility is set to selected. You can add and remove individual repositories using the Add selected repository to an organization secret and Remove selected repository from an organization secret endpoints.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Replaces all repositories for an organization secret when the visibility for repository access is set to selected. The visibility is set when you Create or update an organization secret.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

secrets.title: Add selected repository to an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Adds a repository to an organization secret when the visibility for repository access is set to selected. For more information about setting the visibility, see Create or update an organization secret.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

response.description: No Content when repository was added to the selected list

statusCodes.description: No Content when repository was added to the selected list
Conflict when visibility type is not set to selected

secrets.title: Remove selected repository from an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Removes a repository from an organization secret when the visibility for repository access is set to selected. The visibility is set when you Create or update an organization secret.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

response.description: Response when repository was removed from the selected list

statusCodes.description: Response when repository was removed from the selected list
Conflict when visibility type not set to selected

secrets.title: List repository organization secrets

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Lists all organization secrets shared with a repository without revealing their encrypted values.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

secrets.title: List repository secrets

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Lists all secrets available in a repository without revealing their encrypted values.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

secrets.title: Get a repository public key

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Gets your public key, which you need to encrypt secrets. You need to encrypt a secret before you can create or update secrets.

Anyone with read access to the repository can use this endpoint.

If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

schema.description: The public key used for setting Actions Secrets.

parameters.name: secret_name

parameters.name: owner

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name

secrets.descriptionHTML:

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read secrets.

variables.title: List organization variables

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 30). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

variables.descriptionHTML:
Lists all agent variables available in an organization. Returned variables include their values.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

created_at.description: The date and time at which the variable was created, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

updated_at.description: The date and time at which the variable was last updated, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

variables.title: Create an organization variable

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: name
bodyParameters.description: The name of the variable.

bodyParameters.name: value
bodyParameters.description: The value of the variable.

bodyParameters.name: visibility
bodyParameters.description: The type of repositories in the organization that can access the variable. selected means only the repositories specified by selected_repository_ids can access the variable.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository ids that can access the organization variable. You can only provide a list of repository ids when the visibility is set to selected.

variables.descriptionHTML:
Creates an organization agent variable that you can reference in a GitHub Actions workflow.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

variables.title: Get an organization variable

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

variables.descriptionHTML:
Gets a specific agent variable in an organization.

The authenticated user must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

created_at.description: The date and time at which the variable was created, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

updated_at.description: The date and time at which the variable was last updated, in ISO 8601 format':' YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

variables.title: Update an organization variable

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

bodyParameters.name: name
bodyParameters.description: The name of the variable.

bodyParameters.name: value
bodyParameters.description: The value of the variable.

bodyParameters.name: visibility
bodyParameters.description: The type of repositories in the organization that can access the variable. selected means only the repositories specified by selected_repository_ids can access the variable.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository ids that can access the organization variable. You can only provide a list of repository ids when the visibility is set to selected.

variables.descriptionHTML:
Updates an organization agent variable that you can reference in a GitHub Actions workflow.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

variables.title: Delete an organization variable

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

variables.descriptionHTML:
Deletes an organization agent variable using the variable name.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

variables.title: List selected repositories for an organization variable

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

variables.descriptionHTML:
Lists all repositories that can access an organization agent variable that is available to selected repositories.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

advanced_security.description:
Enable or disable GitHub Advanced Security for the repository.

For standalone Code Scanning or Secret Protection products, this parameter cannot be used.

dependabot_security_updates.description: Enable or disable Dependabot security updates for the repository.

status.description: The enablement status of Dependabot security updates for the repository.

reviewers.description: The bypass reviewers for secret scanning delegated bypass

reviewer_id.description: The ID of the team or role selected as a bypass reviewer

reviewer_type.description: The type of the bypass reviewer

mode.description: The bypass mode for the reviewer

custom_properties.description: The custom properties that were defined for the repository. The keys are the custom property names, and the values are the corresponding custom property values.

statusCodes.description: Response when the visibility of the variable is not set to selected

variables.title: Set selected repositories for an organization variable

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: The IDs of the repositories that can access the organization variable.

variables.descriptionHTML:
Replaces all repositories for an organization agent variable that is available to selected repositories. Organization variables that are available to selected repositories have their visibility field set to selected.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

statusCodes.description: Response when the visibility of the variable is not set to selected

variables.title: Add selected repository to an organization variable

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

variables.descriptionHTML:
Adds a repository to an organization agent variable that is available to selected repositories. Organization variables that are available to selected repositories have their visibility field set to selected.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Response when the visibility of the variable is not set to selected

variables.title: Remove selected repository from an organization variable

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

variables.descriptionHTML:
Removes a repository from an organization agent variable that is available to selected repositories. Organization variables that are available to selected repositories have their visibility field set to selected.

Authenticated users must have collaborator access to a repository to create, update, or read variables.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint. If the repository is private, the repo scope is also required.

statusCodes.description: Response when the visibility of the variable is not set to selected
